assertions.text: V1 was in lane one of a two-lane, one-way roadway
V2 was legally parked on the north side of the same roadway
The front plane of V1 contacted the back plane of V2 (V1 struck V2 from behind)
V1 final rest position was in the roadway
V2 final rest position was in the roadway
There was one impact between V1 and V2
No braking by V1 is asserted in the narrative

verdicts.evidence: The claim that V1 was traveling westbound cannot be determined from the available telemetry: vehicle [speed 34.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s and 32.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2) is recorded but heading or GPS bearing is not provided in get_window_info (T1), so direction (westbound) is [unverifiable](T1).

Lane-level location (V1 was in lane one of a two-lane, one-way roadway) is not available in the telemetry. The data show vehicle speeds [34.0 km/h and 32.0 km/h](T2) and steering small deviations [peak -5.0 deg at t=-5.0 s](T2) but no lane or lateral position or map data are present in the window info (T1), so this is [unverifiable](T1).

Whether V2 was parked on the north side and was legally parked cannot be inferred from the V1 telemetry. The EDR shows a crash event with [max longitudinal delta-V of -18.0 km/h at t=0.16 s](T1) and vehicle count recorded as [1 vehicle in crash](T1), but no information about V2's location or parking legality is present in the telemetry (T1), so this claim is [unverifiable](T1).

Telemetry shows a cumulative longitudinal delta-V of [final -18.0 km/h with max -18.0 km/h at t=0.16 s](T2) and EDR summary lists [max_delta_v_long_kmh -18.0](T1). The negative longitudinal delta-V indicates V1 decelerated (consistent with being struck at the front by contacting the rear of another vehicle), supporting that V1's front contacted V2's rear (V1 struck V2 from behind) as described.

Final rest position of V1 being in the roadway is not recorded in the telemetry. The sensors record crash pulse [delta-V final -18.0 km/h](T2) but contain no final GPS position or rest-location data in the provided channels (T1), so this is [unverifiable](T1).

V2 final rest position being in the roadway cannot be determined from V1 telemetry. The EDR notes [vehicles_in_crash 1](T1) and crash pulse [delta-V -18.0 km/h](T2) but gives no location of V2 after the impact, so this claim is [unverifiable](T1).

The EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded 1](T1) and the crash pulse data show a single longitudinal delta-V peak with [max_abs_value 18.0 km/h at t=0.16 s and final_value -18.0 km/h](T2), consistent with one impact being recorded between V1 and another object, supporting that there was one impact between V1 and V2.

The narrative asserted no braking by V1, but the telemetry shows a brake transition to ON at [t=-0.5 s] and a brake fraction_on of [0.18] over the pre-crash window (T2), contradicting the claim that no braking occurred.

result.rationale: EDR telemetry clearly shows a frontal-decoupling crash pulse with [max_delta_v_long_kmh -18.0](T1) and a single recorded event [num_events_recorded 1](T1), supporting an impact from ahead; however, many location and contextual narrative claims (heading, lane, V2 parking and rest positions) are not present in the V1 telemetry and thus unverifiable. Brake channel shows a pre-impact application at [t=-0.5 s](T2), contradicting the narrative's implicit no-brake assertion.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
